Nurturing the greatness in every child - and in you.

 

Join one of Australia’s Largest Leading Early Education Providers!  

At G8 Education, we’re on a mission to shape the future! With 350+ early education and care centres under 21 brands, our 7,000+ dedicated team members nurture over 40,000 children every day.  

As an ASX listed growth company, we’re committed to empowering, rewarding, and supporting every team member – from our centre educators to our leadership and support team.  

The Opportunity 

We're looking for an experienced Product Manager to join our Digital Solutions team.

Reporting to the Head of Enterprise Architecture, you will own the strategy, roadmap and ongoing performance of G8's Finance Technology portfolio, ensuring our finance systems continue to support the evolving needs of the business.

Working closely with Finance, and external vendors, you'll be responsible for the operational performance of our finance technology services while identifying opportunities to enhance capability, improve user experience and deliver measurable business value.

This is a highly collaborative role that requires someone who can confidently engage senior stakeholders, lead technology change initiatives and balance strategic planning with operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities

  • Own the product strategy and roadmap for G8's Finance Technology portfolio.
  • Ensure the effective operation and continuous improvement of finance technology services across the organisation.
  • Partner with Finance and business leaders to understand operational needs and translate them into technology solutions.
  • Lead product discovery, prioritisation and delivery of enhancements that drive business value.
  • Monitor market trends and emerging technologies, identifying opportunities to strengthen the product strategy.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders and external technology vendors.
  • Lead change initiatives across the Finance Operations portfolio, ensuring successful adoption of new capabilities.
  • Manage product performance, budgets, priorities and success metrics to maximise business outcomes.

About You 

You will be a commercially minded Product Manager with strong finance systems experience and a passion for delivering technology solutions that improve business performance.

  • Strong understanding of finance and accounting processes.
  • Demonstrated experience managing Finance solutions including Back Office General Ledger (GL) systems.
  • Proven ability to influence and manage relationships with senior business stakeholders.
  • Experience managing third-party technology vendors and strategic partnerships.
  • Strong experience leading business and technology change initiatives.
  • Experience developing product strategies, roadmaps and continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving and communication skills.
  • Experience with Coupa and Microsoft Dynamics 365 (D365) will be highly regarded.
  • A tertiary qualification in Business, Information Technology, Finance or a related discipline.
  • Hold a valid working with children check or willingness to obtain upon commencement.

Benefits at G8

  • Competitive salary package
  • Eligibility to participate in our Short-Term Incentive program which is an annualised bonus each year
  • Hybrid working conditions with 3 days in office and 2 from home.
  • Monthly RDO
  • Located in Varsity Lakes.
  • Career development opportunities as part of a large ASX-listed company 
  • Access a 50% discount on early education and childcare fees at any G8 Education Centre. 

Find out more here: G8 Education

United by purpose. Guided by values.

At G8 Education, we’re proud to celebrate, support, and inspire our 7,000+ team members as they make a lasting difference in the lives of children and their own.

Driven by our purpose “to nurture the greatness in every child to grow, thrive and learn” and guided by our values, we’re shaping bright futures for children, families, communities, and each other.

Our values: Safety First & Always, Thriving Together, Owning the Outcome, Being the Difference, Building Bright Futures.

Our Recruitment Process

Recruitment Process

Our recruitment process includes police checks and verification of qualifications and right-to-work status.

Please note that the advertised closing date is indicative only, and applications may close earlier. We encourage you to apply as soon as possible.


G8 Education is a committed advocate for child protection, safety and wellbeing. We have a zero tolerance for any form of child abuse or harm. The safety and wellbeing of children will always be our priority, and all team members are expected to embrace this shared commitment.

About our Organisation 

VENTORA is the name behind iconic Australian brands including A&L Windows & Doors, Breezway louvres, Corinthian Doors, Stegbar Windows & Doors and Trend Windows & Doors.

As an established manufacturer with over 4,500 employees, our vision is to inspire our customers through leading brands, great service, innovative new products and solutions leveraging the full VENTORA portfolio, while significantly enhancing our ease of doing business built on operational excellence.

About the Opportunity

Due to internal promotion, we are now seeking our next Plant Technology Manager to oversee and manage the IT operational technology environment. In this pivotal role you will ensure that all systems are up to date, fit for purpose, reliable, secure and efficient. As the bridge between operational technology (OT) and IT, you will ensure seamless integration, collaboration and alignment of technological goals with business objectives.

Responsibilities include:

  • Oversee the implementation, maintenance and optimisation of IT operational technology systems across all business units, including multi-function devices and barcode scanners
  • Ensure seamless integration between enterprise IT systems (ERP, CRM) and OT systems (SCADA, MES)
  • Support the convergence of IT and OT environments to enable data-driven decision making and predictive maintenance strategies
  • Work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment with operational and business objectives
  • Develop and enforce technology standards, policies and procedures
  • Participate in operational technology projects from conception to completion, ensuring timely delivery in adherence to defined standards and policies
  • Implement robust cyber security measures to protect assets and data
  • Conduct regular testing, auditing and assessments

About You

 

While ideally you will bring similar experience from a manufacturing environment, what is vital is your natural ability to communicate and work effectively with multiple stakeholders and cross functional teams.

You will also have:

  • Diploma or above in IT (ideal but not essential)
  • A good understanding of networking, cyber security and general IT infrastructure
  • Knowledge of manufacturing processes and related technology systems eg. SCADA, PLCs, MES (highly regarded)
  • Previous experience with remote access systems
  • Previous experience using InTune
  • The ability to work with and support multiple sites across multiple time zones

About the Benefits

 

  • Competitive remuneration
  • Flexible work arrangements, with 2 days per week WFH
  • Generous discounts on our extensive range of products
  • Ability to schedule leave every year over the Christmas period.
  • Additional paid day’s leave on your birthday
  • Flare benefits program - salary packaging, discounts and premium perks
  • Employee Assistance Program access
  • Career progression opportunities as part of a 4,500-employee organisation

Your Next Steps

Apply now to become VENTORA’s new Plant Technology Manager and help drive operational excellence!

Eligible applications must have legal rights to work full time in Australia.

Agencies will only be engaged if required. Please do not contact us or send applicants to us unless requested by the Talent Acquisition Team.

Curious about VENTORA Group? Check out our website at https://www.ventora.com.au for comprehensive insights into our operations. Prefer visuals? Watch our short overview video here: https://www.youtube.com/@VENTORA_GROUP

At Downer we plan, create and sustain. Downer is the leading provider of integrated services in Australia and New Zealand. Through trusted relationships and world leading insight we work closely with our customers to design, build and sustain assets, infrastructure, and facilities.

The Opportunity

Downer's Energy & Utilities business unit has an opportunity for a Project Manager to join our Metering team working with Local Councils. Reporting to the National Operations Manager, this role is responsible for the successful delivery and performance of contracts and projects.

The role oversees project planning, financial performance, compliance, stakeholder management, risk management, safety and resource allocation to ensure contractual obligations are met safely, efficiently and profitably.

As the primary point of accountability for contract delivery, you will ensure timely delivery in accordance with client expectations, legislative requirements and organisational standards.

What we are looking for

  • Demonstrated experience managing contracts and/or projects, including project budgets, forecasting and financial performance
  • Background in Water, plumbing or metering preferred
  • Strong client relationship and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build positive relationships with clients, contractors, suppliers and internal stakeholders
  • Strong understanding of project management methodologies and principles
  • High-level communication and reporting skills
  • Ability to lead, coach and support multidisciplinary teams to achieve business objectives
  • Strong planning, organisational and problem-solving abilities
  • Experience within the Metering industry is desirable

What we offer

  • Opportunity for career growth with structured frameworks and supportive-inclusive team
  • Competitive Salary and Employee Benefits
  • Valued and respected position, with supportive and experienced team
  • Work for a major ASX-listed company

About Energy & Utilities…Downer's Energy & Utilities business unit is an integrated trans-Tasman team, providing services and solutions that connect communities to essential networks and infrastructure. We design, build, operate and maintain today's critical assets and networks, delivering complete services across the water, electricity, telecommunications, industrial and power generation sectors. Our expertise spans in-house technical smarts, specialist engineering, integrated OEM partners, and dedicated crews of qualified tradespeople.

Downer Group is an employer that acknowledges and embraces the importance of our people's diverse experiences, talents, and cultures. As an inclusive employer, we welcome applications from everyone.

How to apply

Click "Apply" to start your application. We look forward to hearing from you!

Downer does not accept unsolicited resumes, fees, or costs from recruitment agencies, search firms, or third parties. Candidates are welcome to submit their applications via the advertisement.

Technical Lead

What's in it for you?

· Join a leading organisation delivering innovative digital solutions using modern cloud technologies.

· Lead technical decision-making while remaining hands-on across Azure infrastructure and full-stack development.

· Work with cutting-edge technologies including Azure, .NET, React, TypeScript and AI-assisted engineering tools.

· Enjoy a hybrid working model with opportunities to influence technical direction and mentor engineers.

Role

We are seeking an experienced Technical Lead to provide technical leadership across the full software development lifecycle, including analysis, design, development, testing, deployment and support. This is a senior hands-on role that combines Azure cloud engineering with full-stack software development. You will work closely with senior engineering stakeholders to deliver secure, scalable and high-performing applications while driving engineering best practices and mentoring the development team.

Employment Type and Duration

  • Permanent

Work Model

  • Hybrid (3 days per week in the office)

Location

  • Broadbeach, Queensland

Key Responsibilities

· Design, build and manage Azure infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code (Bicep), Container Apps, API Management, Key Vault, networking and landing zones.

· Develop and maintain full-stack applications using .NET, React and TypeScript.

· Build and maintain API integrations with internal and external platforms.

· Lead the adoption of AI-powered engineering tools such as Claude Code and GitHub Copilot, establishing best practices across the team.

· Provide technical leadership through code reviews, mentoring and engineering standards.

· Ensure solutions are secure, scalable, highly available and aligned with PCI and data privacy requirements.

· Collaborate with Solution Architects to implement enterprise architecture and technical standards.

· Drive continuous improvement across development practices, DevOps processes and software quality.

Required Skills & Experience

· Proven experience designing, building and operating Azure cloud infrastructure and microservices in enterprise environments.

· Strong hands-on experience with .NET development, REST APIs, React, TypeScript, Entity Framework, SQL and relational databases.

· Minimum 5 years' experience as a Technical Lead or in a similar senior technical role.

· Experience using AI-assisted software engineering tools such as Claude Code, GitHub Copilot or similar.

· Experience with Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, Octopus Deploy and CI/CD pipelines.

· Strong understanding of Agile delivery methodologies (Scrum/Kanban).

· Experience with DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) and observability practices.

· Excellent communication, stakeholder management and technical leadership skills.

· Experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central and AWS will be highly regarded.

Qualifications & Clearance

· Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or a related discipline.

· Microsoft Azure certifications are highly desirable.

· Full Australian working rights.

If you have what it takes to thrive in this role and want to know more, please reach out to Isha Khanna at isha.khanna@akkodisgroup.com

Privacy

Due to new privacy laws regarding the collection and handling of personal information, all applications must be submitted online. Once you click on the "apply for this job" button below you will be directed to our Online Centre where you will have the opportunity to read and agree to our Privacy and Consent statements and it will allow your details to be formally lodged and processed correctly.

"Akkodis is an Equal Opportunity employer, committed to all forms of workforce inclusion. We actively invite and encourage applications by candidates from diverse backgrounds"

Tequila Sunrise Hostel is hiring a Full time Business Analyst role in Surfers Paradise, QLD. Apply now to be part of our team.


Requirements for this role:

  • 2-3 years of relevant work experience required for this role

About Us

We are a rapidly growing hostel and hospitality group operating six properties across Australia and one international property in Guatemala. Our business accommodates thousands of guests each month and employs over 100 staff across operations, reservations, finance, marketing and customer service teams. As we continue to expand, we are seeking an ICT Business Analyst to support the implementation, integration and optimisation of business systems across our group.

The Role - ICT Business Analyst

Reporting to the Directors, the ICT Business Analyst will analyse business processes, identify technology improvement opportunities and deliver system solutions that improve operational efficiency, financial reporting and guest experience.

Key Responsibilities

  • Analyse existing operational and financial business processes across multiple properties.

  • Gather and document business requirements from management and operational teams.

  • Design and implement technology solutions that improve efficiency and reporting.

  • Lead business system implementation and integration projects.

  • Develop dashboards and management reports using operational and financial data.

  • Analyse occupancy, revenue, labour and performance metrics to support decision-making.

  • Coordinate with software vendors and third-party technology providers.

  • Develop functional specifications and business process documentation.

  • Train staff in the use of new business systems and procedures.

  • Identify opportunities for automation and digital transformation.

  • Support system upgrades, testing and deployment activities.

  • Ensure consistency of business processes across all properties.

Skills & Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Business Information Systems, Accounting, Finance or related discipline.

  • Minimum 3 years' experience as a Business Analyst, Systems Analyst, ERP Consultant or similar role.

  • Experience gathering business requirements and translating them into system solutions.

  • Experience preparing functional specifications and business documentation.

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Experience with ERP, finance or hospitality management systems highly regarded.

  • Experience delivering end-user training and stakeholder engagement.

  • Excellent communication and project management skills.

Desirable

  • Experience with SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics or similar ERP systems.

  • Experience in hospitality, accommodation or tourism industries.

  • Experience with reporting and business intelligence platforms.

The role in a nutshell:
  • You will develop strategies to gain new clients, penetrate new markets and gain new business.
  • You will attend networking events, industry functions and conferences to generate new opportunities and build and maintain a robust sales pipeline.  
  • You will conduct discovery meetings to understand customer challenges, business objectives and technology requirements, and maintain a consultative sales approach focused on business outcomes rather than product sales.
  • As this is a newly created role, you will also use your knowledge to build and document procedures and processes internally.
  
  
To be successful, you will need:
  • Previous experience as a business development manager, preferably in a Managed Services Provider environment. You have a demonstrated track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets and revenue goals.
  • You have strong knowledge of the technology sector, including current trends and their business applications.
  • You are a hunter, and enjoy looking for new business and building relationships.  
  • Excellent communication and relationship-building skills and ability to work with technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • You must be based on the Gold Coast, with a valid work permit as my client isn’t able to sponsor a visa.
  
  
  
Why should you apply?
  • This is an opportunity to work with a strong business and a variety of customers, where no 2 days will be the same
  • Located on the Gold Coast: need I say more?
  • This is your opportunity for you to start and build a role from scratch and potentially grow a team.
  
  
  
About our client:
Our client is a managed IT solutions provider, and their priority is to ensure business continuity for their customers. They work closely with businesses to allow them to be more productive through the right utilisation of technology.
Partnership being key, they know businesses and know how critical the stability of their operations is. They provide a variety of technical services including managed services, cloud services, web hosting and development, taking the load of their customers and ensuring their peace of mind. The results are there: over 230 businesses trust them to manage their IT requirements.
This position serves as a crucial intermediary role, acting as a buffer between senior management and daily operational issues.
  
  
  
How to Apply?  
If this sounds like you or for a confidential discussion about the vacancy and your situation, please contact me on Whats’app on +6421 786 160 or email me your CV on chloe@xorecruitment.co.nz . If you are interested in this position but your experience doesn’t align perfectly with every point in the description above, please get in touch anyway! Our client is about building a diverse and inclusive workplace and recognizes that everyone possesses a unique set of skills, knowledge, experience, and that everybody is different and unique! You may just be the right candidate for this role, or any other we may be recruiting now or in the future.  
  
We are a Circle Back Initiative Agency – We commit to respond to every applicant.

Senior Business Analyst – Ignite Travel Group (Broadbeach)

We are looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join the Ignite Engineering team in Broadbeach!

Ignite Travel Group is an award-winning travel company (NTIA Most Outstanding Online Travel Agency 2022), and Australia’s largest travel advertiser, delivering incredible holidays and trailblazing win-win-win models for our people, customers, and business partners.

 

About the role

 

As a Senior Business Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in capturing business requirements and ensuring the successful implementation of our new travel booking platform, alongside supporting the backlog for our existing back-office platform. You will work closely with stakeholders to understand their needs, identify opportunities for improvement, and provide clear requirements to the engineering team on their behalf. Our engineering team is embracing AI-assisted delivery, and you’ll bring experience and confidence with adopting AI tools — this is not a developer role, but these tools are central to how you’ll analyse, specify and validate requirements – and you’ll help shape how we use them.

 

Key responsibilities:

  • Gather and document business requirements, translating them into user stories and acceptance criteria within an agile software delivery team.
  • Develop use cases and process flows to support the development of software solutions
  • Use AI tools to accelerate requirements analysis and produce clear, well-structured specifications
  • Manage and refine the product backlog across both our existing back-office platform and our new travel booking platform, ensuring priorities reflect business value
  • Conduct stakeholder interviews to understand business needs and pain points
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to ensure that project objectives align with business goals
  • Facilitate communication between business and technical teams
  • Participate in user acceptance testing and ensure that solutions meet business requirements
  • Develop and maintain system documentation and training material
  • Providing leadership within the project teams to ensure delivery of customer requirements and expectations

 

Who you are:

  • Experience within travel or other internet-scale environments desirable and strong understanding of the travel or retail industry
  • Extensive proven experience in key business analysis discipline areas including: requirements elicitation and documentation (as user stories, use case and acceptance criteria), process analysis and documentation, and organisational strategy
  • Experience and confidence working with AI-assisted analysis and specification
  • Curiosity and adaptability — willingness to continuously learn new tooling and ways of working as our practices evolve
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Knowledge in business process re-engineering and improvement
  • Well-developed written and oral communication and facilitation skills
  • Ability to conduct virtual meetings and workshops
  • Excellent documentation skills, both technical and non-technical
  • Attention to detail and effective organisation and time management skills
  • Strong business acumen and critical thinking skills
  • 5+ years' experience in a similar business analyst role
  • Business Analyst formal qualification highly desirable
  • Familiarity with spec-driven developing practices highly regarded; (producing specifications precise and unambiguous enough to drive both engineering teams and AI coding agents)
  • Experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central highly regarded
  • UI/UX experience highly regarded

 

 

What you'll enjoy:

 

📚 Flexible working opportunities 

🚀Ongoing professional development and skill sharpening

🚀 Catapult your career across a network of brands and businesses in an evolving industry

✈️ Say no to full price! Nab discounts on travel and more thanks to our sweet rewards program

👩‍⚕️ Access to industry leading health and well - being programs and financial advisors

🌴 Set up shop in a swanky beachfront office in Broadbeach

💃🏿 Enjoy social events to promote networking, celebration of wins, and sometimes just for fun!

 👐🏻 Take volunteer leave as part of giving back to the local community

 

We value you... 

Ignite Travel Group is committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace that supports your unique identity to create better, safer experiences for everyone. We encourage you to come as you are; to foster inclusivity and collaboration. We celebrate you.

ABOUT MASTERY SCHOOLS AUSTRALIA:

A small, not-for-profit Middle School (Years 4-10) dedicated to teaching and supporting students who are disengaged or at risk of disengaging from mainstream schooling due to learning difficulties or academic stress. We provide delivery of the full Australian Curriculum, including the general capabilities with a focus on explicit instruction pedagogy (Direct Instruction) / Mastery Teaching.

For more information on us, please visit www.msa.qld.edu.au

ABOUT THE ROLE:

We are seeking a part-time Junior Software Developer to join our growing team. This role is ideal for a university student currently studying Computer Science or a related field who is looking to gain hands-on industry experience while continuing their studies. We are looking for someone with a genuine interest in software development, strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently when investigating technical challenges.

We value candidates who have built personal projects, maintain a GitHub portfolio, or can demonstrate practical experience through projects. Experience with Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, APIs, databases, Git, or cloud platforms such as AWS or Azure is beneficial but not essential. We are particularly interested in applicants who can explain the technical decisions behind projects they have built, demonstrate how they have solved difficult problems.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

Responsible for assisting in the development, testing, deployment, and maintenance of software applications, automation tools, and internal business systems. The role contributes to software development projects, system integrations, and business process automation initiatives.

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS:

  • Currently studying Software Engineering, Computer Science, IT, Cyber Security, or a related field

  • Genuine interest in software development

  • Strong problem-solving ability and willingness to investigate issues independently

  • Good written communication skills

  • Attention to detail

  • Reliable and able to follow through on tasks

  • Able to self-manage and meet deadlines

  • No adverse findings resulting from criminal history check (i.e. AFP National Police Check for working with children or no unsuitable findings by the Department National Investigations Unit)

  • Hold a current and valid Working with Children Blue Card

If you are looking to start a career and make a difference in the world, please apply through this ad or send through your resume and cover letter to hr@msa.qld.edu.au

• Full Time Fixed Term (2x positions available until 30/06/2027) | 9-day fortnight | 36 hours per week
  • Salary: From $111,401 - $117,924 per annum plus Superannuation
  • Position Brief - Solution Designer

About the role

We are excited to offer an opportunity for an innovative and delivery-focused Solution Designer to join the City of Gold Coast.

Reporting to the Coordinator Solution Architecture, you will provide expert advice on solution design best practice and lead technical designs that align with reference solutions, enterprise architecture principles and the City’s enterprise standards. You will develop clear, implementable designs and system specifications that support the successful delivery, transition and operation of ICT solutions across the organisation.

Depending on organisational priorities, you may be aligned to one of several activity streams. This could include supporting ICT design across municipal infrastructure projects, leading cross-platform initiatives under the IT Operating Model, or contributing to complex ICT portfolio initiatives that require strong governance, technical alignment and operational readiness. Across each stream, your focus will be on ensuring solutions are well defined, practical, compliant, and sustainable in service.

You will work closely with project teams, technical specialists, delivery partners, vendors and stakeholders across the Chief Technology Office and the broader City. You will coordinate technical input, manage design dependencies, support transition to operations, and help uplift consistent technical solution architecture patterns and practices that improve the way technology is delivered across the organisation.

This is a highly collaborative role suited to someone who can translate business needs into clear technical outcomes, balance design quality with delivery realities, and bring people together to solve complex problems. Your contribution will help ensure technology solutions are fit-for-purpose, aligned to enterprise standards, and capable of supporting services that enhance the Gold Coast way of life.

We’re looking for people who have:

Most importantly, you will bring experience in a similar Solution Designer or Solution Architect role, with the ability to turn business needs into clear, practical and implementable technology designs. You will be confident working across technical teams, project teams, vendors and business stakeholders to shape solutions that are aligned to enterprise architecture, City standards and operational requirements.

To succeed in this role, you will bring:

  • Demonstrated experience developing solution designs, system specifications and implementation-ready design artefacts that align with enterprise architecture principles, reference solutions, technical patterns and organisational standards.
  • Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • A practical delivery mindset, with the ability to balance design quality, compliance, risk, operational readiness and delivery realities.
  • The ability to coordinate technical input across multiple teams, manage dependencies, support transition to operations, and contribute to reliable and consistent solution delivery.
  • Experience working across one or more delivery environments, such as municipal infrastructure projects, cross-platform service initiatives, ICT portfolio delivery, system migrations, operational uplift or technology-enabled business improvement.
  • Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work with a high level of autonomy while contributing positively to cross-functional teams.
  • An understanding of ICT risk, service transition, operational support considerations, vendor engagement and the importance of designing solutions that are sustainable beyond project delivery.
  • A continuous improvement mindset, with an interest in uplifting technical patterns, improving solution design practices, and helping the City deliver fit-for-purpose technology outcomes for the community.

Ideally, you will also hold a Bachelor Degree in Information Technology, Project Management or a related discipline, or bring equivalent knowledge and relevant experience.

If you have strong solution design capability and can bring people together to deliver practical, standards-aligned technology outcomes, we’d like to hear from you.

Be part of shaping the Gold Coast’s future

The Gold Coast is inspired by lifestyle and driven by opportunity. We are the second largest Council in Australia.

We care about being the most flexible government organisation in Australia. We have a range of benefits to help you create the right balance:

  • 9-day fortnight

  • Personal and professional development courses
  • Access to 350+ fitness facilities through Fitness Passport

We pride ourselves on our shared mission of being a high-performing, customer-focused organisation which delivers value-for-money services to the community.

To submit your application, please click apply now and complete the application form. You will be required to upload your resume and a cover letter (no more than two pages) outlining how your skills, experience and personal attributes will enable you to be successful in this role. Please note that you may also be asked to provide relevant qualifications, where applicable.

CKA Windows & Doors, Yatala | Full-time role | $75,000 - 85,000 annual salary

CKA Windows & Doors is a growing commercial aluminium windows and doors business working across residential and commercial projects throughout South-East Queensland. 

Role Overview

The Business Analyst will work closely with cross-functional teams to identify business needs, document requirements, analyse processes, and support the delivery of technology solutions that improve business performance, system functionality, and user outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

·         Engage with business users and stakeholders to elicit, analyse, and document business and system requirements.

·         Review current business processes, procedures, systems, and workflows to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities.

·         Translate business needs into clear functional specifications, user stories, process maps, and system requirements for technical teams.

·         Work with stakeholders to support solution design and delivery.

·         Use data and process modelling techniques to define current state and future state business processes.

·         Support project planning activities, including scope definition, resource estimates, risk identification, implementation planning, and system integration.

·         Assist with system testing, user acceptance testing, defect management, and quality assurance activities.

·         Prepare user documentation, training materials, procedures, and support guides for new or changed systems.

·         Provide advice and act as a central reference point for business and system-related decisions throughout the project lifecycle.

·         Support change management activities to ensure successful adoption of new technology solutions.

Required Skills and Experience

·         Preferred demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst, Business Systems Analyst, or similar role.

·         Strong ability to gather, document, and validate business requirements with diverse stakeholders.

·         Experience preparing functional specifications, business process models, use cases, user stories, and acceptance criteria.

·         Understanding software development lifecycle methodologies, including Agile and/or Waterfall approaches.

·         Strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, and facilitation skills.

·         Ability to work collaboratively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.

·         Experience with system testing, user acceptance testing, and implementation support.

·         High attention to detail and ability to manage competing priorities in a project environment.

·         Experience with the Microsoft ecosystem including SharePoint, Power BI and Power Automate.

Qualifications

·         Bachelor’s degree or higher qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, Business Information Systems, Business Analysis, or a related discipline is preferred.

·         Experience working in manufacturing, construction, or a related industry is advantageous.

Rapid LED Pty Ltd have an excellent opportunity for a full-time Web Administrator to join our team.

The successful candidate will be responsible for:

  • Designing, building, maintaining and continually improving the company's production websites and internal web applications that run our daily logistics operations — freight quoting, booking, dispatch, delivery tracking, warehouse labelling and operational reporting — keeping them reliable and responsive as the needs of our dispatch, warehouse and delivery teams change.

  • Installing, configuring and administering web server software and cloud hosting for our Python (Flask) and Node.js (Express) web applications, including deployments, uptime and health monitoring, and performance tuning.

  • Assessing and specifying the software and hardware needed to keep the web systems running and to resolve operational problems, and installing, configuring and integrating the required applications and tools.

  • Configuring and maintaining the connectivity that links the web systems — HTTPS/SSL, cross-origin access, single sign-on, server-to-server API connectivity, and the network links to on-site label printers.

  • Administering and maintaining the PostgreSQL databases that support these web applications, including schema changes, data migrations, backups and data-integrity controls.

  • Administering, maintaining, enhancing and troubleshooting the REST API integrations between our web applications and external platforms — shipping carriers, ERP, mapping and communication services — including webhooks.

Applicants for this position should:

Remuneration

$85,800 per annum plus superannuation.

Applications open

This position is open to applications and expressions of interest for a minimum 28 days from the date of publication.

Make ten people faster, not one dashboard prettier.

VERVE Fitness is one of Australia's fastest growing commercial and home gym equipment brands. We're profitable, we run lean, and we're further down the AI road than almost any business our size: autonomous marketing agents, AI customer service, a company work OS, scheduled agents doing real jobs every day. The plumbing exists. The gap is people.

Our finance, sales and marketing teams are busy people with real work piling up, and most of them use a fraction of what's already built for them. We need someone whose whole job is closing that gap: sit next to a real person, understand the work they hate, automate it with AI tools, and, this is the part most people fail at, make sure they're still using it a month later.

What you'll actually do

  • Embed with named people, one at a time. Finance, sales, marketing, and the CEO's own workflows. You watch how they work, find the hours being wasted, and delete those hours.

  • Build small and sharp. Python scripts, API integrations (email, CRM, ERP, spreadsheets), Claude-powered workflows. We already have a large working codebase of automations to build on; you extend the estate, you don't rebuild it.

  • Train and embed. Teach non technical people to use AI tools properly. Your wins are measured in their adoption, not your output. A perfect tool nobody opens counts as zero.

  • Kill things. Every automation you ship has usage telemetry and a review date. If it isn't used, you kill it and learn why. We've had tools rot before; your job includes making sure they don't.

You're probably right for this if:

  • You're an operator technologist, not a software engineer: strong Python and API skills, but your instinct is to fix a workflow, not to architect a platform.

  • You use Claude, GPT or similar daily and know how to turn "AI could probably do this" into a thing that runs every morning without being babysat.

  • You're patient and likeable with non technical people. A finance officer who's nervous about AI has to enjoy working with you, or nothing you build survives.

  • You measure yourself in hours deleted and weekly active users, and you're comfortable being judged that way.

The deal

  • $600 to $750/day depending on experience, 3 days/week on the Gold Coast, potentially expandable to 4-5 days, 3-month initial engagement.

  • If the queue is real and the adoption numbers are real, we'll talk about a permanent role ($90k to $120k + super). If four weeks of good work clears the backlog, we part as friends. We're telling you that upfront because it's the truth.

  • Direct access to the CEO, who is himself a heavy AI user. You will not spend your days explaining what an API is to your boss.

How to apply

Send a CV plus a short note describing one automation you built that a non-technical person still uses today: what it replaced, how you know they still use it, and what you'd do differently. That second question matters more than your CV.

Own a whole product. Make it bulletproof. Launch it.

VERVE Fitness is Australia's fastest-growing commercial gym equipment company. We're profitable, we're on the Gold Coast, and over the past year we've built something new: VERVE Pulse, a gym management platform. Memberships, payments, bookings, member apps for iOS and Android, front-desk kiosk, lead management. It's built, it's in trial with real gyms, and it's about to go to market.

We're hiring our first dedicated engineer to own it. Not a team. Not a department. You, the product, and the best AI development tooling money can buy. Internally the title is Founding Engineer, and we mean it: first and only engineer on the product.

What the job actually is

This role changes shape as the product launches, and we're telling you that upfront:

  • First 2 months: break it before customers do. Systematic, hostile testing of every critical flow. Payments above all: real cards, full billing cycles, refunds, failed payments, dunning, reconciliation to the cent. You find it, you fix it, it stays fixed.

  • Months 3 to 4: run the customer beta. Real gyms, real members, real money. You triage what they hit, fix fast, and keep a clear-eyed picture of what's solid and what's risky. The CEO makes the launch call on your evidence.

  • From launch: it's yours in production. Uptime, incidents, security, the features customers are asking for, and an onboarding machine you build so every gym is cheaper to bring on than the last. If it breaks at 9pm, you're the person who cares.

How we build

The codebase is modern (Next.js, React, Postgres on Supabase, Vercel, React Native, Stripe) and it was built AI-first, with Claude Code doing much of the writing and a human doing the thinking. That's how you'll work too. One engineer here ships what a five-person team used to. If you've been using AI coding tools and thinking "this changes everything", you'll fit. If you think it's a fad, you won't.

You're probably right for this if:

  • You're a senior full-stack engineer who has owned software in production: carried the pager, handled the 9pm payments incident, written the post-mortem.

  • You've worked with Stripe or similar payments infrastructure in anger: webhooks, retries, reconciliation, invoicing. You know where the bodies are buried.

  • You have taste for what "done" means when real money moves. You'd rather delay a launch than ship a billing bug.

  • You use AI coding tools daily and you're good at catching what they get wrong.

  • You want ownership of one product, not a slice of a big org chart.

What you get

  • Competitive salary depending on experience.

  • End-to-end ownership of the product: the codebase, the ship rhythm, the quality bar. Inside a profitable company, so no funding rounds and no runway anxiety.

  • Direct line to the CEO. Decisions in hours, not quarters.

  • The Gold Coast. Work where other people holiday.

How to apply

Apply with your CV and a short note answering one question: describe a production incident you personally owned, what caused it, and what you changed so it couldn't happen again. No cover letter needed. We move fast: phone screen, technical deep dive, paid half-day work sample, offer.

In this role, you will lead the design, architecture and delivery of innovative AI Business Solutions across Microsoft Dynamics 365, Power Platform, Azure and emerging technologies.

You will combine technical leadership with hands-on solution delivery, working closely with clients to solve complex business challenges while mentoring a high-performing team of developers and consultants.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the end-to-end architecture, design and delivery of enterprise solutions across Dynamics 365, Power Platform, Azure, and AI technologies.

  • Act as the senior technical escalation point, providing expert guidance on complex solution challenges.
  • Mentor and support Senior and Mid-level Developers, promoting best practice development and architectural standards.
  • Collaborate with clients and stakeholders to deliver innovative technology solutions that drive measurable business outcomes.
  • Stay at the forefront of Microsoft technologies and AI innovation, identifying opportunities to enhance client solutions and internal capabilities.

Skills Required

  • Extensive experience designing and delivering Microsoft Dynamics 365, Power Platform and Azure solutions.
  • Advanced expertise in Power Automate, including Desktop Flows, Power Apps, Dataverse and Azure integration patterns.
  • Experience integrating Microsoft technologies with third-party platforms, APIs and cloud services.

Senior Business Analyst | Digital Products | Agile | AI-Enabled Delivery
Gold Coast | Hybrid

About the Company

St Hilda's School is one of Australia's leading independent Anglican schools, educating students from Pre-Prep to Year 12, including boarding. Situated on the Gold Coast, the School is recognised for its commitment to academic excellence, and student wellbeing.


Built on strong Anglican values, St Hilda's encourages leadership, service and lifelong learning. Through innovative teaching practices, extensive co-curricular opportunities and a supportive community, students are equipped with the knowledge and character to succeed in an increasingly digital and interconnected world.

Guided by its motto, Non Nobis Solum ("Not for Ourselves Alone"), the School promotes integrity, responsibility and a genuine commitment to making a positive contribution to society.

About the Role

St Hilda's School is seeking an accomplished ICT Manager to lead the strategic direction, governance and operational performance of the School's technology environment.

Reporting to the Director of Business and Operations, this role provides leadership across all aspects of ICT, ensuring technology services enable outstanding educational outcomes while supporting the School's administrative and operational functions.

The position extends well beyond traditional ICT management. As a trusted adviser to senior leadership, the ICT Manager will shape the School's digital future by driving innovation, strengthening cybersecurity, enhancing data governance and supporting the responsible adoption of emerging technologies, including Artificial Intelligence.

Leading a dedicated ICT team, the successful candidate will foster a culture of service excellence while ensuring the School's technology ecosystem remains secure, resilient and aligned with strategic priorities.

The ICT Manager will:

  • Develop and run the School's technology strategy, ensuring ICT initiatives support broader organisational objectives.
  • Lead the management and optimisation of core technology infrastructure, including networks, cloud services, Microsoft 365, Windows and Apple device environments.
  • Strengthen cybersecurity capability through effective governance, risk management and data protection practices.
  • Support the responsible adoption of emerging technologies, including Artificial Intelligence, while contributing to long-term digital innovation.
  • Oversee the delivery of responsive ICT support services that provide an exceptional experience for staff and students.
  • Manage relationships with external technology partners, including procurement, licensing, vendor performance and contract negotiations.
  • Collaborate with academic leaders to enhance digital learning environments and classroom technology outcomes.
  • Ensure appropriate disaster recovery, business continuity and system resilience measures are maintained.
  • Lead, coach and develop the ICT team, fostering collaboration, accountability and professional growth.
  • Manage technology budgets, project delivery and the successful implementation of new systems and initiatives.

About You

You are a strategic technology leader who enjoys balancing long-term planning with operational excellence. You understand that technology exists to enable people and outcomes, and you thrive in environments where collaboration, innovation and service are equally important.

You'll bring:

  • Demonstrated success leading ICT teams within a complex organisational environment, with education sector experience viewed favourably.
  • Broad technical knowledge spanning enterprise infrastructure, networking, cloud platforms and end-user computing.
  • Practical experience implementing cybersecurity frameworks, managing organisational risk and protecting critical information assets.
  • A proven record of delivering technology projects, digital transformation programs and enterprise system implementations.
  • The ability to move comfortably between strategic planning and operational execution.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to build trusted relationships and explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
  • Experience managing ICT budgets, supplier relationships and commercial contracts.
  • A commitment to delivering high-quality customer service and driving continuous improvement.

Overview

We're hiring engineers who build things customers actually use. The title says "backend," but what we want first is a product-minded problem solver who happens to be excellent at backend systems. We’re looking for someone who cares more about whether a feature ships and works than about which framework got them there. The systems we build are real-time, mission-critical, and genuinely complex.

Our backend runs primarily on Java/JVM.

How We're Organized

We don't have product managers. At the senior and staff level, you talk to customers directly, understand their problems, decide what to build, and ship it. That's not a side responsibility; it's the job.

In practice, that means:

  • Prioritization happens at the initiative level. Engineering is organized into a small number of initiatives, each with a named owner and tech lead who set direction and resolve roadmap conflicts. You're not prioritizing in a vacuum,  but you do own what gets built inside your area.

  • Saying no is part of the job. Customers and internal stakeholders will ask for things. Part of being senior here is having the judgment to push back, propose alternatives, or escalate when trade-offs need a wider conversation.

  • You're supported on customer work, not thrown into it. Customer success, sales engineering, and support handle the front line. When you join a customer call, it's because the problem needs an engineer in the room, not because you're running the account.

  • Conflicts go to the tech leads and CTO. Disagreements about architecture, scope, or priority don't fester. We resolve them quickly, in writing, with clear owners.

If you've worked in environments where PM hand-offs slowed you down or watered down the product, you'll recognize why we do it this way. If you've never owned the full loop before, this will be a stretch — and that's fine, as long as you're hungry for it.

Working with Agentic Tools

Agentic development has changed what senior engineering looks like day-to-day. We're not throwing out craft. If anything, the bar on judgment, architecture, and verification has gone up. But the mechanics have shifted: more time spent specifying problems clearly, reviewing generated code with a critical eye, and catching the subtle failure modes that agents miss. Less time on boilerplate.

The engineers doing the best work here treat agents the way a strong senior treats a sharp but inexperienced teammate: delegate the right things, verify carefully, and own the outcome. They also understand that LLM intelligence is jagged — brilliant in some places, naive in others — and have built the instincts to know when to trust the output and when to dig deeper.

We want engineers who've already adopted these tools, formed real opinions about where they help and where they don't, and can bring that experience to the team.

What You'll Do

  • Work directly with customers to understand problems worth solving — and decide which ones to solve

  • Design backend systems and APIs that scale to billions of transactions

  • Use agentic tools effectively to implement features, while holding a high bar on what ships

  • Own architecture and trade-off decisions in your domain (we use a Bronze/Silver/Gold blast-radius framework — you'll learn it fast)

  • Debug production issues across services, including the gnarly ones that require deep system understanding

  • Champion reliability, observability, and blast-radius awareness in everything you ship

  • Raise the bar in code review — for both human-written and agent-generated code

  • Mentor teammates on customer empathy, spec-driven development, and getting real leverage from agents

You Might Be a Great Fit If You...

  • Lead with product instinct. You ask "should we build this?" before "how do we build this?" — and you're comfortable getting on a call with a customer to find out.

  • Want to own the full loop: customer problem → spec → implementation → ship → follow-up.

  • Are fluent in Java/JVM — or fluent enough to read, debug, and verify Java code at a senior level

  • Have integrated agentic tools (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) into your daily workflow and have grounded opinions about what works and what doesn't

  • Are inquisitive by default — you read papers, try new tools, talk to customers, and bring ideas back to the team

  • Take ownership. When something breaks, you fix it. When something is unclear, you go find out.

  • Have e-commerce, payments, or international trade background — or are excited to go deep in the space

Here at Zonos we are committed to cultivating a team of ambitious and innovative people who are hungry and willing to stretch and grow. International trade is complicated and working at a growing cloud technology company is hard, but we are here to support your learning and development. For these reasons, if you are uncertain about applying, we encourage you to do so even if your experience doesn't exactly match the job description. We welcome diverse perspectives from people who think outside the box and aren't afraid to challenge assumptions. Come join us!

This is a remote position.

THE SPONSOR:

Bloom Equity Partners is leveraging decades of investing and operating experience to rapidly unlock transformational growth and deliver superior returns to our investment partners and management teams.  Investing exclusively in lower-middle market technology, software and tech-enabled business service companies, Bloom drives enduring market value by partnering closely with founders and management teams, injecting capital to unlock growth and providing operational resources and expertise to enable meaningful step-change to the business.

As we continue to grow and establish our presence in the market, we are seeking an experienced and forward-thinking System Integration Engineer to join our team. The ideal candidate will be based in the United States or Canada and be passionate about driving the financial success of our business.

JOB SUMMARY:

RightCrowd’s System Integration Engineer plays a key role in our project delivery team by implementing our solution and coordinating integration with other products. Collaborating with our customers, through the project from building initial technical requirements to installation and user acceptance testing, the System Integration Engineer ensures a smooth roll out for the customer. Leading the technical relationship with the customer, this role helps manage scope, direction, and technical delivery of the project.

ESSENTIAL RESPONSIBILITIES AND DUTIES:

•                 Perform user acceptance testing using solid organizational skills including attention to detail and multi-tasking skills.

•                 Perform implementations and configuration of systems.

•                 System Administration and Active Directory troubleshooting in Windows focusing on SQL Server and IIS.

•                 Level 3 Support and mentorship for more junior members of the team.

Requirements

KNOWLEDGE/SKILLS/ABILITIES:

●       Strong knowledge of software and hardware systems and troubleshooting techniques.

●       Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

●       Solid experience with:

o   Microsoft Windows Server

o   MS SQL Server T-SQL and internals

o   Active Directory

o   At least two scripting languages

o   C#, JAVA, .Net

o   Networking

o   IIS

●       Scripting experience with one or more scripting languages including SQL, JAVA or Powershell.

●       Active Directory Administration and Integration Experience

As a Network Support Analyst at Queensland Airports Limited, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the seamless operation of our critical network infrastructure across all QAL-managed airports. Your expertise will be instrumental in maintaining network performance, troubleshooting issues, and implementing improvements to support our dynamic and growing operations.

 

Aryan joined the Technology team in 2025 as a Support Analyst and has since moved into a Cyber Security Analyst position. Aryan shares his unique experience working in the QAL team:

“I joined QAL because of its strong emphasis on professionalism and teamwork. What I value most is the collaborative culture, where team members actively share knowledge and support one another’s growth.

QAL provides me with a supportive and motivating environment, and I am proud to be part of the organisation.”

 

How will you make an impact?

We're open to applications from recent graduates, as well as candidates with up to 1-2 years' experience in a similar role. You'll have strong customer service and communication skills, with the ability to assist employees and provide basic service desk support and issue resolution.

Strong organisational and administrative skills are essential, enabling you to coordinate contractor engagement paperwork, manage competing priorities, and ensure tasks are completed accurately and on time.

 

Key deliverables/responsibilities include but are not limited to:

How we support you

At Queensland Airports Limited (QAL), we believe work should support life, not compete with it. Through our Living Well approach, we offer flexible working options for all roles, wellbeing initiatives and a supportive culture so our people can thrive both professionally and personally.

What we’ve built together

At QAL, our people connect communities. We own and operate Gold Coast, Townsville, Mount Isa and Longreach airports, linking regional Queensland and northern New South Wales with the rest of Australia and the world.

More than 8 million passengers travel through our airports each year, supported by teams working together to create safe, welcoming and efficient experiences. Our vision is to be the first-choice gateway for our regions, creating valuable connections and inspiring a sustainable future.

We’re committed to an inclusive and psychologically safe workplace where people feel valued, supported and encouraged to be themselves. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every requirement.

QAL is a WORK180 Endorsed Employer, independently recognised for supporting long‑term careers, flexibility, wellbeing and equal opportunity. Learn more about working at QAL here.

Enterprise Solution Architect - 6 month contract

Are you an experienced Enterprise or Solution Architect looking for the opportunity to build an architecture capability from the ground up?

Limelight People is partnering with a large, Gold Coast based organisation seeking an Enterprise Solution Architect tasked with establishing and embedding a modern architecture practice across its enterprise technology environment.

This is an influential role where you'll develop the frameworks, artefacts and governance that will shape future technology decisions across the organisation. 

About the Role

Working within a collaborative technology division of approximately 60 IT professionals, you'll partner closely with Business Solutions, Operations, Cyber Security and Infrastructure teams to improve the way technology is designed, governed and evolved.

Your primary objective will be to establish the organisation's architecture capability by developing practical frameworks, standards and governance that become embedded into day-to-day IT delivery.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Establish and embed the enterprise solutions architecture practice
  • Develop architecture artefacts, standards, templates and design guidelines
  • Build and maintain architecture principles, reference architectures and decision frameworks
  • Assess and report on enterprise systems health, lifecycle, technical debt and technology risk
  • Improve visibility of application, integration and service dependencies
  • Establish pragmatic architecture governance and Design Authority processes
  • Define integration patterns, API standards and data flow models
  • Support technology projects, procurement activities and strategic investment decisions
  • Coach and mentor technical teams to build architecture capability across the organisation

About You - You'll be a pragmatic architect who enjoys balancing strategic thinking with hands-on delivery.

You'll ideally bring:

  • Microsoft Azure and Microsoft 365
  • ServiceNow or enterprise ITSM platforms
  • Cyber security architecture and governance
  • Experience within large, multi-stakeholder organisations
  • Extensive experience as an Enterprise Architect or Senior Solution Architect
  • Experience establishing or maturing an architecture practice within a complex enterprise environment
  • Strong knowledge of enterprise applications, SaaS, cloud platforms and integration architecture
  • Experience developing architecture frameworks, standards and governance models
  • Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence technical and executive audiences
  • Experience assessing application portfolios, system health, lifecycle management and technology risk
  • Familiarity working within Agile delivery environments

Please apply if you're passionate about building architecture capability, improving enterprise technology landscapes and influencing strategic technology outcomes.

The Opportunity

We have an exciting opportunity for an experienced Business Analyst to join our Digital, Technology & Innovation team and play a central role in delivering SEE Group's digital transformation program. You'll bridge the gap between business needs and technology solutions - engaging stakeholders across the business, capturing and analysing requirements, mapping current and future-state processes, and translating these into clear, well-documented specifications that guide the design and delivery of new systems and enhancements.

Working across a broad application landscape spanning enterprise systems, Microsoft cloud platforms, and bespoke applications, you'll help ensure the solutions we deliver are fit for purpose, adopted successfully, and deliver measurable value to the business.

Responsibilities

  • Engage stakeholders through workshops, interviews and observation to gather, analyse and prioritise business, functional and non-functional requirements

  • Document current-state (as-is) processes and design improved future-state (to-be) processes, identifying gaps and opportunities to streamline operations

  • Translate business requirements into clear functional specifications, user stories and acceptance criteria

  • Build strong working relationships with stakeholders at all levels, acting as a trusted link between the business and technical teams

  • Develop test scenarios and coordinate user acceptance testing (UAT), tracking and resolving defects and feedback prior to release

  • Support the delivery team throughout implementation and help the business adopt change through user documentation, training materials and process guides

Essential Skills and Attributes

  • Previous experience as a Business Analyst or similar role, ideally within construction, engineering, mining or a related industry

  • Demonstrated experience in requirements elicitation, analysis and management, with the ability to produce clear specifications, user stories and acceptance criteria

  • Skilled in process mapping and documentation using techniques such as BPMN or process flow diagrams

  • Comfortable using data to support analysis and decision-making; familiarity with PowerBI or the Microsoft data stack is a plus

  • Sound understanding of enterprise business applications and the Microsoft cloud ecosystem (Microsoft 365, Power Platform, Azure)

  • Experience working within Agile and/or Waterfall delivery methodologies, with hands-on use of tools such as Azure DevOps (Boards)

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts in plain language

  • Tertiary qualification in Business, IT or Information Systems, or equivalent practical experience (desirable); BA certification such as BABOK/IIBA or Agile/Scrum (desirable)

The SEE Way

SEE Group is a family-built company with over 35 years of experience in delivering excellence across civil construction, mining, and infrastructure. At SEE Group, we don't just build projects—we build careers.

  • Be part of a down-to-earth, supportive team that values diversity and inclusion

  • Benefit from career growth and tailored development opportunities

  • Enjoy personalised rewards and recognition for your hard work

  • Work on impactful projects that shape communities

  • Collaborate with passionate people who bring a "boots & all" attitude to everything they do

What's Next?

If you would like to join our team and hold full, unlimited working rights in Australia, we encourage you to submit your CV by clicking apply now.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, Women and People of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Island heritage are strongly encouraged to apply.

Interested to learn more about SEE Group? Visit our website at www.seegroup.com.au

At the Gold Coast Convention and Exhibition Centre, technology powers everything we do.


From major conferences and exhibitions to live entertainment and corporate functions, our IT team delivers reliable, high-performing services in an environment where downtime is not an option.

We are seeking an experienced and operationally focused Service Operations Lead to oversee the delivery of event technology support and corporate IT services across the Centre.

This is a hands-on leadership role suited to someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, enjoys solving operational challenges, and can confidently coordinate technology delivery across multiple competing priorities.

 

THE ROLE

Reporting to the IT Manager, you will lead day-to-day IT service operations across the business, with responsibility for service delivery, event readiness, incident management, operational coordination, and service quality.

You will act as the operational lead for IT services during live events, ensuring technology services are well planned, properly resourced, and executed to a consistently high standard.

The role also plays an important part in maturing IT operational practices across the organisation, including helping define, implement, and embed standardised ITIL-aligned service management processes across both corporate IT and event delivery environments.

Your key responsibilities will include:

  • Leading the delivery of event technology support and corporate IT services
  • Coordinating IT planning and operational readiness for approximately 110 events annually
  • Managing live event IT delivery and responding to operational issues in real time
  • Overseeing service desk operations, incidents, requests, and workload prioritisation
  • Leading major incident response and problem management activities
  • Helping define, implement, and continuously improve ITIL-aligned operational processes across the organisation
  • Introducing and embedding standardised practices across areas such as incident management, problem management, change management, service request management, and operational readiness
  • Driving service quality, operational consistency, and continuous improvement initiatives
  • Developing and maintaining operational processes, runbooks, and readiness procedures
  • Working closely with internal stakeholders, vendors, AV teams, and event organisers
  • Supporting and mentoring operational team members while promoting strong service standards

 

ABOUT YOU

You are an experienced IT operations or service delivery professional who can combine technical understanding with strong operational leadership.

You are calm under pressure, highly organised, communicate well with nontechnical people and can effectively coordinate service delivery in environments where priorities shift quickly and reliability matters.

To be successful in this role, you will ideally bring:

  • Experience in IT operations, service delivery, or technical operations leadership
  • Strong understanding of IT infrastructure, networking, cloud, and end-user technologies
  • Experience working within ITIL-aligned service management environments
  • Proven ability to coordinate operational delivery across multiple priorities
  • Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
  • Experience improving operational maturity, governance, or IT service management practices within complex environments will be highly regarded
  • Experience supporting event, hospitality, venue, entertainment, or high-availability environments will be highly regarded
  • Cisco, Microsoft, and ITIL certifications well regarded

 

If you are a service-focused IT operations professional looking for a role with variety, responsibility, and real operational impact, we would love to hear from you.

 

WHY WORK WITH US?  “It’s not what we do, it’s how we do it”! 

 

We pride ourselves on our values of Community, Initiative, Respect, Teamwork and Welcoming.  These values sit at the heart of how we work, how we treat our people, represent our business and deliver outstanding events. 

Plus, we offer the following people perks:

  • Free undercover parking in the heart of Broadbeach
  • Discounted onsite team member café
  • Flexible work arrangements
  • Paid parental leave
  • Discounted food and beverage and accommodation at The Star Entertainment Group properties
  • Access to health & wellbeing programs
  • Ongoing learning and development opportunities
  • Reward & Recognition Program

 

Reaching Earthcheck Master Certification status we are an environmentally conscious and sustainability focused organisation that seeks to continuously educate, support and ensure our team members foster and understand the importance of our environmental stewardship.

 

Full Australian working rights are required for this position in addition to a probity check.

 

The Gold Coast Convention and Exhibition Centre is part of The Star Entertainment Group.

To apply, please upload your cover letter and resume.

At the Gold Coast Convention and Exhibition Centre, technology powers everything we do.


From major international conventions and exhibitions to concerts, gala dinners and corporate events, our infrastructure must be reliable, secure and ready to perform every day.

We're looking for an experienced Senior Platform Engineer to join our Technology team and help build, maintain and continually improve the infrastructure platforms that support both our corporate operations and live event environments.

If you're passionate about modern infrastructure, cloud technologies, automation and continuous improvement, we'd love to hear from you.

 

THE ROLE

Reporting to the IT Manager and working under the technical direction of the Platform Engineering Lead, you'll play a key role in implementing, maintaining and improving the technology platforms that underpin the organisation.

You'll work across infrastructure, cloud, identity, networking, endpoint management and venue technology, ensuring our platforms remain secure, resilient and highly available.

This is a hands-on technical role where you'll have the opportunity to contribute to infrastructure modernisation, automation, cybersecurity improvements and platform standardisation while supporting one of Australia's most dynamic technology environments.

Your key responsibilities will include:

  • Building, configuring and maintaining infrastructure platforms across cloud, networking, identity, endpoint and venue technologies
  • Implementing standard platform designs and engineering best practices
  • Maintaining platform reliability through proactive monitoring, patching, upgrades and lifecycle management
  • Implementing and maintaining security controls while supporting vulnerability remediation and security improvement initiatives
  • Developing automation that improves deployment consistency, operational efficiency and platform reliability
  • Providing Level 3 technical support for complex infrastructure and platform incidents

  • Preparing and validating technology platforms supporting live event delivery
  • Developing and maintaining technical documentation, standards, runbooks and knowledge articles
  • Contributing to the ongoing evolution of GCCEC's technology platforms and engineering standards

 

ABOUT YOU

You're an experienced infrastructure or platform engineer who enjoys solving complex technical challenges and takes pride in delivering secure, reliable and well-engineered technology solutions.

You have strong technical capability but also understand the value of documentation, standards, automation and continuous improvement.

To be successful in this role, you'll ideally bring:

  • Experience implementing and supporting enterprise IT infrastructure and platforms
  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft Azure, Windows Server, Active Directory/Entra ID and Microsoft endpoint technologies
  • Strong networking knowledge including switching, routing, wireless and network segmentation in enterprise, service provider or campus environments
  • Experience implementing security controls and supporting cybersecurity best practice
  • Experience with scripting or automation technologies such as PowerShell or similar tools will be highly regarded
  • Experience with backup and disaster recovery platforms
  • Experience supporting high-availability, hospitality, venue or event environments will be highly regarded
  • Relevant certifications such as Microsoft, Azure, Cisco, Palo Alto Networks, Security or ITIL are all well regarded

 

If you're an experienced infrastructure or platform engineer looking for a technically rewarding role with variety, responsibility and the opportunity to work on modern enterprise technologies, we'd love to hear from you.

We pride ourselves on our values of Community, Initiative, Respect, Teamwork and Welcoming.  These values sit at the heart of how we work, how we treat our people, represent our business and deliver outstanding events. 

Plus, we offer the following people perks:

Reaching Earthcheck Master Certification status we are an environmentally conscious and sustainability focused organisation that seeks to continuously educate, support and ensure our team members foster and understand the importance of our environmental stewardship.

 

Full Australian working rights are required for this position in addition to a probity check.

 

The Gold Coast Convention and Exhibition Centre is part of The Star Entertainment Group.

To apply, please upload your cover letter and resume.

VDS Contract Manufacturing is a fast growing manufacturing business of pharmaceutical and food supplements located on the Gold Coast. VDS Contract Manufacturing, manufactures according to TGA, cGMP, HACCP & ACO requirements. Our experienced team strive to achieve optimal customer satisfaction through taking a personal approach in your manufacturing journey, constructing your concept into a reality. VDS Contract Manufacturing endeavors to create continual exceptional rapport with new and existing customers with emphasis on trust, honesty and respect. Our experienced and friendly team will help you along the way to manufacture the finest product on the market, paying attention to detail and leaving customers delighted with the final result. At VDS Contract Manufacturing customer satisfaction is crucial and we aim to exceed expectations.


The Role

The team at VDS is seeking reliable production (encapsulation) operators to work in their production team. We are seeking for operators who can work from Monday to Friday or at least 4 days a week.

Your daily task will require you to work on production lines where you will be assisting in manufacturing individual capsule supplements.

We are especially interested in candidates with experience in encapsulation.


Tasks & responsibilities

  • Work closely with room leaders to constantly deliver on quality food products.

  • Follow batch documents and internal procedures.

  • Conduct quality checks and in-process checks

  • Identify and report equipment or quality issues promptly

  • Must be able to work independently

  • Any other task as required in the production process


Qualifications & experience

  • Previous experience in a manufacturing or GMP-regulated environment

  • Experience working in encapsulating machines

  • Must have attention to detail

  • Mechanically minded individuals preferred

  • Willingness to learn a new skill

Benefits

GMP Rules:
