Telecommunications Field Engineer

We are seeking an experienced Telecommunications Field Engineer to join our team. The role involves hands-on field work supporting the design, installation, commissioning, and maintenance of telecommunications networks, including fibre optic and other carrier infrastructure systems.

You will play a key role in ensuring reliable network performance, diagnosing complex faults, and supporting the delivery of high-quality telecommunications projects in the field.

Key Responsibilities

  • Plan, design, and support telecommunications network infrastructure and associated equipment.

  • Install, commission, and monitor telecommunications networks, including fibre optic systems.

  • Conduct testing, fault finding, and performance evaluation of network equipment and infrastructure.

  • Identify and resolve complex technical issues in telecommunications networks.

Skills & Experience

  • Minimum 2 years’ experience in telecommunications on-field required.

  • Strong understanding of telecommunications systems, structured cabling, and network infrastructure.

  • Ability to interpret technical drawings, schematics, and network plans.

  • Hands-on experience with testing, commissioning, and fault diagnosis of network systems.

  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to manage complex technical issues.

  • Good communication skills and ability to work with clients and team members.

Mandatory Tickets & Certifications required

Highly Desirable

What We Offer

jobs.job_title: Field Inspection and Maintenance Assistant - Brisbane North
jobs.job_description:
We're an iconic Aussie brand with a global footprint. Our purpose is to build a connected future so everyone can thrive. We're all about providing the best experience and delivering the best tech on the best network.

Looking for a hands-on career where you can make a real impact? We're encouraging First Nations peoples to apply for Field Inspection and Maintenance Assistant roles that help connect and support communities across Australia. You'll receive training, mentoring, and be part of a team that values your skills, experience, and story.

Current locations in demand: QLD - Brisbane North

About the role
As a Field Inspection and Maintenance Assistant, you will work without immediate supervision or direction to provide our customers with a great experience by inspecting, servicing, and maintaining Network Assets or other elements of the Telecommunication Network.

You will actively participate in a diverse team environment to deliver the optimum customer and commercial outcomes aligned to the corporate strategy.

We have several positions available around the country. Find the specific locations below. The expectation is to reside in the targeted area or be willing to relocate.

This offer is a 12-month fixed term contract with opportunity for extension.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Deliver seamless Network rectification and remediation where required.
  • Inspect and maintain Network Assets such as Poles, Payphones, Pits, and Exchange components.
  • Ensure work is undertaken in a timely cost-effective manner, work efficiently and at a high standard to meet the performance targets set.
  • Adhere to Health, Safety and Wellbeing standards, our Code of Conduct, our Telstra Values and Corporate policies.


Qualifications:
You will be required to hold and maintain the relevant and applicable licenses and qualifications to perform this role, as deemed mandatory by regulatory and legal authorities and must also maintain a current driver's license.

  • A valid car licence
  • An industry white card
  • Traffic management qualifications (beneficial)
  • Working with Children Check


Telstra can support you through obtaining a Working With Children Check and White Card.

Why Telstra?
We're committed to creating a workplace that honours diversity and inclusion. That means real flexibility, inclusive policies, and leadership that walks the talk.

What We Offer

  • Everything from head to toe. From the vehicle, tools, and equipment through to the full uniform, we provide it all.
  • 8-hour shifts, Mon-Fri. Overtime when required.
  • 12 Month Fixed Term Contract. With the opportunity for permanent contract thereafter.
  • Industry recognised training & unlimited learning. Take part in industry recognised training courses and level up your skills with access to 17,000 on-demand learning programs.
  • Extra perks. Receive 30% off Telstra products and services, plus unlock exclusive discounts from over 240 brands and partners.
  • Plus so much more!


Inclusion Matters Here
We're stronger when we reflect the communities we serve. At Telstra - we're committed to creating opportunities for First Nations peoples to pursue thriving careers with Telstra. Some of our Supportive policies include;

  • Cultural Leave. Enjoy dedicated cultural leave to participate in significant events - because your heritage and community connections matter.
  • Flexible Work Options. Flexible work options tailored to your lifestyle and culture, including remote work - because how you work should reflect who you are.
  • First Nations Employee Representative Group. To provide support, mentoring, foster understanding and strengthen connection with First Nation Australian Communities.
  • Deadly Early Years Yarning Space. A space for connection, storytelling and peer support.


We have zero tolerance for harassment of any kind, and we prioritise creating a workplace culture where everyone is safe and can thrive.

We work flexibly at Telstra, talk to us about what flexibility means to you. When you apply, you can share your pronouns and /or any reasonable adjustments needed to take part equitably during the recruitment process.

If this opportunity sounds like a perfect fit for you, we'd encourage you to apply!

ABOUT STACKED FARM

Stacked Farm is revolutionizing fresh produce as Australia's first end-to-end automated indoor vertical farming company. Operating across Australia (Gold Coast and Melbourne) and the United States, we combine robotics, automation, software and engineering to produce premium leafy greens year-round. Technology is at the heart of everything we do, from enterprise IT infrastructure and cloud services through to operational technology (OT), industrial networking and cybersecurity.

As we continue to grow, we're looking for a passionate technology enthusiast to join our team and build a rewarding career in IT.

ABOUT THE ROLE

The Junior IT Systems Administrator provides essential IT support across our multi-site operations, ensuring day-to-day technology systems, hardware and software operate reliably.

This role combines IT support, endpoint provisioning and junior systems administration, making it an excellent opportunity for someone early in their IT career who wants to develop into a Systems Administrator.

Reporting to the Chief Technology Officer, you'll provide responsive user support while progressively taking ownership of systems administration, infrastructure and networking responsibilities. As your skills develop, you'll gain exposure to operational technology (OT), cloud and on-premises infrastructure, cybersecurity, enterprise networking and IT infrastructure design.

No two days are the same, and you'll work alongside experienced technical professionals in an environment where you'll continually learn and develop your skills.

RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Provide responsive Level 1 and Level 2 IT support through our ticketing system, troubleshooting hardware, software and user issues across multiple sites.

  • Configure, deploy and maintain laptops, desktops, mobile devices and peripherals.

  • Provision and manage user accounts, access permissions and endpoint devices as part of team member onboarding, offboarding and role changes.

  • Assist with the administration of Windows and Linux servers, virtualisation platforms and remote monitoring and management systems.

  • Support Google Applications and other core business applications.

  • Assist with network administration including switching, routing, VLANs, VPNs, wireless networking and firewall management.

  • Support operational technology (OT) networking, including network segmentation, access control and industrial network hardening.

  • Participate in cybersecurity activities including patch management, endpoint protection, vulnerability remediation and security best practices.

  • Maintain IT documentation, network diagrams, asset registers and standard operating procedures.

  • Assist with hardware procurement, lifecycle management and software licensing.

  • Contribute to infrastructure improvements, automation initiatives and technical projects as your experience grows.

ABOUT YOU

You're passionate about technology and enjoy solving problems. Whether you've gained experience through work, study, certifications or personal projects, you're always looking to learn something new.

You take pride in providing excellent customer service, enjoy troubleshooting technical issues and are excited by the opportunity to grow into a Systems Administrator role.

REQUIREMENTS

  • Diploma, Advanced Diploma or Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Network Administration or a related discipline (or currently completing).

  • Experience in IT support, helpdesk or junior systems administration is advantageous.

  • Solid understanding of networking fundamentals including TCP/IP, subnetting, VLANs, routing, switching and firewall concepts.

  • Working knowledge of Google applications and basic Linux administration.

  • Understanding of virtualisation concepts and hypervisor technologies such as VMware or Hyper-V.

  • Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills with a logical approach to problem solving.

  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users.

  • High attention to detail and strong organisational skills.

  • Proactive, reliable and able to work both independently and collaboratively.

  • Genuine passion for technology with a strong interest in networking, systems administration and infrastructure.

  • Australian working rights.

  • Must be based on the Gold Coast.

Company Overview 

Veracross unifies school communities with world-class information systems that improve the quality of education and enhance learning. We support K-12 private and independent schools with a one-record platform that connects school operations, communication, and data across departments. Trusted by more than 3,200 schools in over 60 countries, Veracross is known for reliable software, strong data security, and high-quality customer support. 

In Australia, New Zealand, and the wider Asia Pacific region, Veracross supports 800+ schools across Veracross, Digistorm, Firefly, and ePraise. The APAC team is based in Australia and offers in-time-zone access through Support, Implementation, and Customer Success, alongside regional technical and product staff. 

Role Summary 

The CSM Team lead, manages the Asia Pacific Customer Success team and is accountable for team performance across retention, qualified opportunity generation, and SIS lead creation. This role owns team coaching, churn forecasting, renewal execution, and operational consistency across the regional portfolio. 

The CSM Team Lead directly leads a team of three Customer Success Managers covering the APAC book, which spans roughly 800 customers across Australia, New Zealand, and Asia Pacific international schools. The book is concentrated in the Australian and New Zealand independent and Catholic school markets, with a smaller set of higher-value international school accounts. This is a high-volume regional portfolio, and the CSM Team Lead is accountable for building coverage discipline and team capacity against it. 

Partnering closely with Sales, Support, Implementation, Product, and leadership to reduce churn, expand customer value, and build a high-performing team. This role may retain a very limited strategic portfolio, but its primary focus is team leadership and regional execution. 

Job Responsibilities 

  • Lead, coach, and develop the APAC CSM team across Australia, New Zealand, and Asia Pacific accounts.
  • Set clear expectations tied to retention, qualified opportunity generation, SIS lead creation, and account management discipline. 
  • Own regional churn forecasting and lead regular risk reviews so risks are identified early, documented clearly, and actively mitigated. 
  • Manage span-of-control and coverage in a high-volume book, balancing account distribution across the team and surfacing capacity and coverage gaps to leadership with recommendations. 
  • Step into high-risk or high-value customer situations as needed to support save plans, renewals, and executive alignment. 
  • Ensure renewals are managed proactively, forecasted accurately, and documented cleanly in Salesforce. 
  • Drive expansion discipline by coaching CSMs on opportunity identification, lead quality, and effective partnership with Sales, including Digistorm cross-sell and Magnus and Firefly motions where relevant. 
  • Enforce consistent use of Salesforce and Catalyst.io for health monitoring, risk tracking, task management, and account planning. 
  • Partner cross-functionally with Sales, Support, Implementation, and Product to resolve escalations and improve customer outcomes. 
  • Hire, onboard, manage, and develop talent, including succession planning for future Team Leads and Managers. 
  • Provide regular reporting and recommendations on regional risk, team performance, process gaps, and staffing needs. 

Requirements

  • Bachelor's Degree or equivalent experience. 
  • 3-5 years of experience in SaaS customer success, account management, or other post-sales roles. 
  • 2+ years of experience in management position 
  • Demonstrated success driving retention, renewals, and expansion performance. 
  • Strong experience with churn forecasting, pipeline management, and operational rigor. 
  • Demonstrated ability to manage a high-volume book or team coverage model, where the challenge is prioritisation and coverage across many accounts rather than deep focus on a few. 
  • Strong coaching, performance management, and cross-functional leadership skills. 
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
  • Ability to operate strategically while staying close to execution details. 
  • Knowledge of the Australian and New Zealand independent and Catholic school markets strongly preferred, including familiarity with the academic calendar (four terms, late-January to mid-December school year), school leadership structures, and associations such as AISNSW, AHISA, and state Catholic education offices. Experience in K-12, education SaaS, or a related niche market is preferred; candidates from outside the sector must show evidence of learning the unwritten rules of a specialised market quickly. 
  • Comfortable operating across time zones and currencies, supporting both AUD/NZD domestic accounts and a smaller set of USD-billed international school accounts. 

Benefits

The best tools to get the job done!

When you join Veracross, you get a Macbook Pro, a 32-inch monitor, and an adjustable desk and chair for the office. There's also an allowance for your setup at home to ensure you're comfortable working there.

Growth opportunities!

Veracross is growing fast, allowing you to own your role and grow with us! Operating within a global team, there are opportunities for professional development allowing your skills to grow as the business grows.

Work/life balance

Management encourages taking hour-long lunches and finishing on time and understands that you have a life outside of work. You can take the day off for your birthday and get a $100-a-year allowance for well-being. Massage, anyone?

Parental leave policy

As part of Veracross parental leave policy, you’re eligible for paid leave as a primary parent and secondary parent when you welcome a new child into your family. This is available after 12 months in a full-time role.

Flexibility

Veracross offers a flexible working environment, hybrid options.

Make a difference

Our work makes a positive contribution to our schools, their students and their families around the world!

Snacks, drinks & coffee

Digistorm’s (Veracross) Gold Coast HQ offers a coffee machine, snack bar and fully stocked drinks fridge to keep energy levels high! Vege chips, kombucha, almond milk, cookies; you name it, they have it.

Culture

The Veracross team is fun, relaxed, and sociable, reflected in the casual dress code and social events. The office has an open floor plan, which allows for cross-team communication and a collaborative environment. Join some staff for lunchtime surfing or hit up nearby food spots for a burger by the beach.

jobs.job_title: Founding Engineer – Full-Stack (Node.js, Angular, AWS & AI)
jobs.job_description:
Tenderfy is redefining how construction and infrastructure companies prepare winning tenders.

We've built a platform that combines great workflows, intelligent automation and AI to eliminate the chaos, duplication and manual effort that has traditionally plagued tendering.

Customers are saving significant time, improving tender quality and changing the way their teams work. We're growing quickly and looking for exceptional people to help build what's next.

The market is responding.

Customers are onboarding, revenue is growing, and we're now looking for a Founding Engineer to help shape the next stage of the company.

This isn't a maintenance role.

This isn't a role where you'll spend your days working through a backlog someone else created.

This is a seat at the table.

You'll have direct influence over architecture, product direction, engineering standards, AI strategy and how we scale the platform over the coming years.

If you're looking for a place where your work genuinely matters, keep reading.

What You'll Be Building

You'll work across the entire platform, including:

  • Backend development using Node.js and TypeScript

  • Modern Angular front-end development

  • AWS infrastructure and cloud architecture

  • AI-powered workflows and automation

  • RAG, vector databases and LLM integrations

  • Document generation and processing pipelines

  • Performance, scalability and platform reliability

  • Integrations with enterprise platforms and third-party systems

  • Internal tooling to improve engineering velocity

You'll work directly alongside the founder and collaborate with our offshore engineering team to rapidly deliver improvements and new capabilities.

What Success Looks Like

Within your first 12 months, you'll:

  • Become a key technical decision-maker within the business

  • Improve platform performance, reliability and scalability

  • Help shape Tenderfy's AI roadmap

  • Strengthen engineering processes and release velocity

  • Contribute to building a world-class engineering culture

  • Play a major role in scaling Tenderfy nationally and beyond

What We're Looking For

We're less interested in years of experience and more interested in capability.

You'll likely have strong experience in:

  • Node.js and modern TypeScript

  • Angular or similar modern front-end frameworks

  • AWS infrastructure and cloud-native development

  • API design and integrations

  • Software architecture and scalable systems

  • AI tooling and modern developer workflows

Bonus points if you've worked in a start-up, scale-up or high-growth environment.

Who You Are

You're commercially minded.

You care about solving customer problems, not just writing code.

You take ownership.

You see problems and instinctively look for ways to improve them.

You actively use tools like Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot and AI-assisted development workflows to move faster and produce better outcomes.

You enjoy building.

You enjoy shipping.

You enjoy learning.

Most importantly, you're excited by the idea of helping build something meaningful from the ground up.

Why Join Tenderfy

  • Fast-moving environment with minimal bureaucracy

  • Work on cutting-edge AI applications

  • Build software used by some of Australia's leading construction and infrastructure businesses

We're building for the long term and we're looking for someone who wants to be part of that journey.

If that sounds like you, we'd love to talk.

Apply with your resume, GitHub profile, portfolio, or simply send through something you've built that you're proud of.

About Buildsafe

Buildsafe is one of Australia's leading construction technology companies, delivering innovative software, AI-powered products and critical infrastructure that help improve safety, productivity and visibility across thousands of construction sites nationwide. Technology is at the centre of everything we do. As our BuildCam platform continues to grow, so does the infrastructure that supports it.

About the Role
We're looking for an
Infrastructure & Systems Administrator to join our IT team, working alongside our Senior Infrastructure Administrator and reporting to the CIO. You'll play a key role in supporting and evolving the infrastructure that powers both Buildsafe and BuildCam.

This is a hands-on role combining enterprise systems administration with physical infrastructure deployment. From commissioning servers, expanding storage and configuring networks to managing Windows Server, Docker, IIS and Microsoft 365 environments, no two days are the same.

Key Responsibilities

  • Build, deploy and maintain enterprise server, storage and network infrastructure.

  • Administer Windows Server, Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, IIS and Microsoft 365.

  • Deploy and manage Docker containers and hosted applications.

  • Monitor infrastructure performance, security, backups and disaster recovery.

  • Configure and support switches, firewalls, VPNs and networking equipment.

  • Troubleshoot infrastructure issues and support branch deployments.

  • Contribute to infrastructure projects, automation, documentation and continuous improvement.

AI-First Culture

AI is embedded in how we work. We encourage our IT team to use AI to automate repetitive tasks, troubleshoot faster, generate documentation, develop scripts and improve operational efficiency. We're looking for someone who enjoys learning, embraces new technology and is always looking for smarter ways to work.

About You

You'll ideally have:

  • 3+ years' experience in Infrastructure or Systems Administration.

  • Strong Windows Server, Active Directory, DNS and Group Policy experience.

  • Experience with IIS, Docker and Microsoft 365 administration.

  • Hands-on experience with enterprise server hardware and NAS/storage platforms (Synology highly regarded).

  • Solid networking knowledge (switches, VLANs, VPNs and firewalls).

  • PowerShell or scripting experience.

  • Strong troubleshooting, documentation and organisational skills.

  • A passion for AI, automation and continuous improvement.

What Buildsafe Can Offer You

Join a scaling division backed by an established national brand, where your contributions genuinely make an impact.

  • Permanent full-time role – salary paid weekly

  • Office based role, Monday to Friday

  • Corporate uniform allowance

  • Fortnightly professional car cleaning on site

  • Regular team bonding experiences

  • Access to our Staff Care program and Employee Assistance Program

  • Discounted gym memberships through Fitness Passport

  • A culture that values innovation, safety, and collaboration

About Us

Buildsafe is one of Australia's leading construction technology companies, delivering innovative software, AI-powered products and critical infrastructure that help improve safety, productivity and visibility across thousands of construction sites nationwide.

Technology is at the centre of everything we do. As our BuildCam platform continues to grow, so does the infrastructure that supports it.

About the Role

Our BuildCam camera system provides a flexible and reliable solution for site monitoring. With 24/7 supervision of residential construction sites, our customers and stakeholders can track developments in real time.

In this newly created role, we are seeking an experienced IT Support Officer to join our expanding BuildCam team. This position plays a key role in maintaining and supporting BuildCam’s technology and IT infrastructure. This position is hands-on, dynamic, and suited to someone who thrives in a problem-solving environment and enjoys working both independently and as part of a collaborative team.

  • Manage system updates, maintenance, and asset tracking

About You

You’ll also bring:

  • Understanding of network setup, connectivity, and troubleshooting

  • Experience conducting quality assurance checks on technical systems

  • Ability to monitor system performance, data integrity, and security alerts

  • Excellent communication and customer service skills

What Buildsafe Can Offer You

  • Access to our Staff Care program and Employee Assistance Program

jobs.job_title: Software Test Automation and DevOps Engineer
jobs.job_description:
An exciting opportunity has become available to join Siemens as a Software Test Automation & DevOps Engineer within our expanding OMVS R&D Team.

This newly created position, based in Yatala QLD, comes at a pivotal time for the business, with continued growth, strong project demand, and ongoing investment in innovative energy infrastructure technologies. Reporting directly to the OMVS R&D Manager, you will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high-quality software solutions across our medium voltage power distribution and protection product portfolio.

This is a hands-on technical role offering exposure to a broad range of technologies including:

  • Embedded firmware for protection and communications devices
  • Desktop applications
  • Mobile applications (iOS/Android)
  • Cloud-based SaaS platforms
  • AWS cloud infrastructure and security environments

Working closely with software development, product management, and customer care teams, you will take ownership across software testing, CI/CD pipelines, release management, cloud infrastructure, and ongoing system reliability. You will thrive in a collaborative environment where teamwork, continuous improvement, and innovation are highly valued, with the opportunity to contribute fresh ideas and help shape the future direction of our products and processes.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Design, develop, and maintain automated test frameworks across multiple platforms
  • Execute both automated and manual testing for software and hardware-integrated systems
  • Build and maintain Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines
  • Manage development, staging, and production deployment environments
  • Drive end-to-end release management and deployment processes
  • Monitor and secure AWS cloud infrastructure environments
  • Implement and improve monitoring, logging, and security practices
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure successful product delivery and software quality

About You:

You are a proactive and solutions-focused professional who enjoys working as part of a team, sharing knowledge, and contributing new ideas. You bring a strong sense of ownership, a collaborative mindset, and a passion for delivering high-quality outcomes in technically complex environments.

You will ideally bring experience across:

  • Automated testing frameworks such as Selenium, Appium, Pytest, JUnit or Robot Framework
  • Azure DevOps and CI/CD pipeline management
  • AWS services including EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, VPC and CloudWatch
  • Python or other scripting/programming languages
  • Git and modern software development practices
  • DevOps, release management, and cloud security principles

Experience within embedded systems, electrical infrastructure, utilities, energy, or industrial technology environments will be highly regarded.

Why Join Siemens?

This is an outstanding opportunity to join a collaborative and forward-thinking R&D team working on technologies that support critical electrical infrastructure globally. You’ll be part of a business experiencing strong momentum in the market, with the opportunity to contribute to innovative products while continuing to grow and develop your career within a globally recognised organisation.

If you are passionate about software quality, automation, DevOps, and cloud technologies - and enjoy working in technically complex environments - we would love to hear from you.

At Siemens, we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to succeed. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, and believe diversity drives innovation and better outcomes for our people, customers, and communities.

Essential Requirements

  • High standard of fibre optic splicing and enclosure preparation

What We Offer

  • A supportive team environment with varied and interesting projects

If you're an experienced fibre splicer who takes pride in your work and enjoys working on critical telecommunications infrastructure, we'd love to hear from you.

IT Service Desk Analyst | Autosports Group – Gold Coast - QLD (National IT Team)

Join a leading automotive retail group supporting dealerships across Australia.

Autosports Group is seeking a proactive and customer-focused IT Service Desk Analyst based in Gold Coast - QLD to join our national IT team. This role supports users across our dealership network, providing responsive and effective technical assistance to ensure seamless business operations.

About the Role

As part of a national IT support function, you will be the first point of contact for technical issues across multiple dealership sites. You will manage service requests, troubleshoot incidents remotely, and deliver end-to-end IT support in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Monitor and manage Service Desk tickets and incoming calls, prioritising workload to ensure timely resolution
  • Provide remote troubleshooting using diagnostic tools and structured problem-solving techniques
  • Deliver professional, end-to-end IT support to users across the national dealership network
  • Manage user access, system permissions, and IT equipment provisioning
  • Maintain clear and consistent communication with users and stakeholders
  • Ensure accurate documentation, tracking, and resolution of all support requests

About You

  • Strong customer service skills with experience in IT support or a genuine passion for the industry
  • Ability to manage competing priorities and resolve issues efficiently
  • Solid understanding of Microsoft Windows operating systems
  • Knowledge of networking fundamentals and ITIL principles (or willingness to learn)
  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to support both technical and non-technical users
  • Highly organised with strong time management and prioritisation skills
  • Able to work independently while contributing to a national team environment
  • Basic understanding of telecommunications technologies
  • Collaborative, adaptable, and solutions-focused

What We Offer

  • Gold Coast - QLD – based role within a national IT team 
  • Modern dealership and office environments
  • Supportive and respectful workplace culture
  • Career development and progression opportunities across a national group
  • Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
  • Family & Friends Referral Program
  • Company car or car allowance

Why Autosports Group?

Autosports Group is a leading automotive retail group representing premium global brands. Our IT team plays a critical role in enabling dealerships nationwide to operate efficiently. This is a great opportunity to build your career in IT while supporting a high-performing national business.

Apply Now

If you're ready to grow your IT career in a fast-paced, national environment, we’d love to hear from you.

ICT Business Development Manager

About the Company

Skyline Infotech Services is a growing IT consulting and digital solutions provider specialising in business process optimisation, systems implementation, managed technology services and digital transformation.

We partner with organisations to identify operational challenges and deliver tailored ICT solutions that improve efficiency, governance, productivity and service delivery.

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced ICT Business Development Manager to lead business development activities, expand our client portfolio and drive the growth of Skyline Infotech Services’ ICT consulting and digital solutions offerings.

The successful candidate will identify new business opportunities, develop relationships with prospective and existing clients, assess client technology requirements and coordinate the preparation of commercially viable ICT solutions.

This role requires a strong understanding of the ICT services market, consultative selling, account development and the commercial application of technology solutions.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement business development strategies for the company’s ICT consulting, systems implementation and digital transformation services.

  • Identify and pursue new business opportunities across target industries and market sectors.

  • Research market conditions, emerging technology trends, competitors and prospective client requirements.

  • Establish and maintain relationships with prospective clients, existing customers, strategic partners and key industry stakeholders.

  • Engage with clients to understand their operational, commercial and technology requirements.

  • Work with technical consultants, solution architects and project teams to develop suitable ICT service offerings and solution proposals.

  • Prepare and present business proposals, capability statements, tenders, quotations and client presentations.

  • Negotiate commercial terms, service arrangements and contractual requirements with clients.

  • Manage the business development pipeline from initial lead generation through to proposal, negotiation and client onboarding.

  • Develop account growth strategies for existing clients, including cross-selling and identifying opportunities for additional ICT services.

  • Establish sales targets, revenue objectives and business development performance indicators.

  • Monitor sales performance, conversion rates, market activity and business development outcomes.

  • Maintain accurate records of client interactions, opportunities, proposals and sales activities using CRM and reporting systems.

  • Represent Skyline Infotech Services at client meetings, networking events, industry forums and business development activities.

  • Collaborate with management to support pricing strategies, service development and broader organisational growth objectives.

  • Provide regular reports and recommendations to senior management regarding market opportunities, client feedback and business development performance.

Skills and Experience

Essential

  • Demonstrated experience in ICT business development, technology sales, account management or commercial development.

  • Strong understanding of ICT consulting, digital solutions, systems implementation, managed services or technology transformation projects.

  • Proven ability to identify business opportunities and develop new client relationships.

  • Experience managing the complete business development lifecycle, including lead generation, client engagement, proposal preparation, negotiation and conversion.

  • Strong consultative selling and solution-development capability.

  • Demonstrated experience preparing proposals, tenders, quotations, presentations and commercial documentation.

  • Excellent stakeholder-management, communication, presentation and negotiation skills.

  • Strong commercial awareness and ability to understand client operational and technology requirements.

  • Ability to collaborate effectively with technical specialists, consultants, project managers and senior management.

  • Proficiency in CRM platforms, Microsoft Office, reporting systems and business-development tools.

  • Strong analytical, organisational and problem-solving skills.

Desirable

  • Experience in ICT consulting, ERP systems, cloud services, workflow automation, cybersecurity, managed services or digital transformation.

  • Existing professional networks within relevant commercial or government sectors.

  • Experience responding to formal tenders, requests for proposals and procurement opportunities.

  • Knowledge of government or corporate procurement processes.

  • Familiarity with Agile, hybrid or technology project-delivery methodologies.

  • Relevant qualifications in information technology, business, marketing, commerce or a related discipline.

Benefits

  • Opportunity to lead business development activities within a growing ICT consulting and digital solutions company.

  • Exposure to a diverse range of ICT, automation and digital transformation projects.

  • Collaborative working environment focused on innovation, commercial growth and client outcomes.

  • Flexible work arrangements, including hybrid working options where appropriate.

  • Professional development and ongoing opportunities to expand industry and technology knowledge.

  • Opportunity to contribute directly to the company’s market strategy and long-term growth.

  • An inclusive workplace that values diversity, initiative and continuous improvement.

How to Apply

To express your interest, please submit your application through SEEK, including your current résumé and a cover letter outlining your relevant ICT business development experience.

Visa sponsorship may be considered for a suitably qualified and experienced candidate who meets all eligibility requirements.

jobs.job_title: IT and Technical Support Specialist - Entry Level
jobs.job_description:
IT and Technical Support Specialist - Entry Level

Part Time | $60K - $70K + Super Pro Rata | Southport

For more than 30 years, InBody has been a global leader in body composition analysis technology, helping people better understand their health through accurate and innovative data. Operating in over 110 countries with more than 1,000 employees worldwide, InBody's technology is trusted by healthcare providers, hospitals, fitness centres, universities, professional sporting organisations, and corporate wellness programs.

As the company expands, InBody Oceania is looking for a motivated and proactive IT and Technical Support Specialist to join their growing team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate, current student, junior IT professional, or service technician looking to develop their career in a supportive and dynamic environment.

This role combines customer service, technical support, field service, and software assistance. Full product and systems training will be provided, making it ideal for someone with a growth mindset and willingness to learn.

About the Role

You will be the first point of contact for customers requiring technical assistance and support. Your responsibilities will include a mix of remote customer support and onsite customer visits across QLD.

Key Responsibilities

  • Respond to customer enquiries via phone, email, and support tickets.

  • Provide software and technical support for InBody products and applications.

  • Assist customers with software integrations and troubleshooting.

  • Liaise with international support teams to resolve technical issues.

  • Support customer onboarding and training.

  • Conduct onsite service visits, maintenance, and device health checks.

  • Assist with installations, commissioning, and equipment setup.

  • Complete service reports and documentation.

  • Build strong relationships with customers and proactively identify service opportunities.

About You

We're open to candidates from a variety of backgrounds, including:

  • IT Support

  • Help Desk / Service Desk

  • Technical Customer Service

  • Service Technician

  • Electronics or Hardware Support

  • Recent Graduates

  • Current Students

To Be Successful, You Will Have:

  • Excellent communication and customer service skills.

  • A proactive and solutions-focused attitude.

  • Basic IT troubleshooting skills.

  • Confidence speaking with customers in person, over the phone, and via email.

  • Strong organisational skills and attention to detail.

  • Ability to work independently and manage priorities.

  • A willingness to learn new systems and technologies.

Highly Regarded

  • Experience in technical support or customer service.

  • Experience working with software applications.

  • Experience with device installations or servicing.

What We Offer

  • Comprehensive training and ongoing support.

  • Exposure to both technical support and field service.

  • Career development and progression pathways.

  • Opportunity to progress into a full-time position.

  • Supportive and collaborative team environment.

  • Diverse and engaging role with no two days the same.

Hours & Location

  • Southport based office.

  • Initially part-time (approximately 24 hours per week across 3 days).

  • Opportunity to transition to full-time as the business continues to grow.

  • Some travel required.


If you're looking for an opportunity to build a career in customer support, technical services, and IT while working with innovative health technology, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply now to join the team at InBody Oceania.

What’s in it for you?
You will be joining a successful, service-based company with a genuine desire to become AI-native and productise their service. This will have all the thrills and opportunities of a startup-style tech environment without the usual funding risks, as it is backed by a well-established, profitable commercial company.

This isn’t just another principal developer job; this is your stepping stone to keep progressing into higher level roles, where you will have the opportunity to come in, work with strong external vendors and be the internal technical lead. You will have hands-on ownership of consolidating the tech stack while serving as the internal technical champion, alongside an elite external development agency, in building the next-generation, AI-native platform.

If you want to move away from rigid corporate bureaucracy, exercise your architectural muscle, and leverage AI tools to move fast, this is your playground. Your footprint will be on every piece of technology that will be scaled globally.


What you’ll be doing:
  • Architecting and executing a repository consolidation project to streamline three legacy platforms (spanning Magento, marketplace, and .NET/SQL Server environments).
  • Serving as the core internal technical authority, partnering closely with the founders, and a top-tier external engineering agency.
  • "Lead the architectural transition to a proprietary, agentic AI-native technology platform, consolidating legacy stacks into a high-performance, modern architecture
  • Balancing deep, rigorous engineering logic with an agile, AI-assisted development approach.
  • Navigating the complexities of legacy codebases to ensure operational stability while the business shifts toward its new flagship platform.

Experience required:
  • Proven experience as a Lead or Principal Engineer, ideally within a scaling startup or fast-paced software environment.
  • Deep technical software engineering experience, with a strong understanding of the software development lifecycle, architecture, and software delivery
  • Strong vendor management skills—you know how to hold external development teams accountable and extract critical technical knowledge.
  • An AI-native mindset, with an intrinsic understanding of how to leverage modern AI tools to accelerate development workflows.
  • Exceptional problem-solving skills and the autonomy required to excel as a self-directed, principal engineer.

About the company:
You are joining a successful, growing service-based business with three established technology platforms, currently scaling into a proprietary, AI-native SaaS model. They are currently investing in building a revolutionary, AI-native SaaS platform designed to automate operations end-to-end, flipping the traditional software model so that AI runs the jobs and humans assist the system.

You will have true autonomy in this role, supported by a strong, well-established leadership team. With a highly profitable core business and a vision to license this software globally, they are poised for explosive growth from their hubs on the Gold Coast and Melbourne.


Benefits:
  • Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) opportunities on the table.
  • A clear, unbureaucratic pathway directly into a senior-level management role as the company builds out its localised engineering team.
  • Flexible hybrid working arrangements, with a strong preference for candidates based near the Gold Coast.

What’s next?
This role is being recruited by NTP Talent. All applicants will receive a response. The easiest way to register your interest is to apply using the link.

Company Description

Are you ready to be a big part of something big?

At Machines4U machinery part of carsales, we’re all about making buying and selling a great experience. We’ve been evolving with the new economy to help people choose how they move today and tomorrow.

When it comes to your career, we want to offer meaningful opportunities that empower you to make a lasting impact. But for that to happen, we strive to feel small, small enough to stay agile, learn across teams, and connect authentically with each other.

With a vision to be the global leader in online vehicle marketplaces, we’re transforming how people buy and sell across the world. We discover new ideas and bring them to life. We continue to grow, and we continue to evolve and we’re not afraid to take bold steps to get there.

A career in Software Engineering will see you working on products that are used every single day by our millions of customers all over the world. This position is within our ACIL (Auto, Corporate, Industry & Leisure) team. The team focuses on high availability product for Dealership customers to buy and sell their inventory. It plays a vital role in allowing them to maximise their online customer interaction and sale funnels.

We embrace hybrid working combining the flexibility of remote work with the energy and connection of in-person collaboration. We champion flexibility and offer a range of policies and leave options to support your wellbeing.

What’s on offer

  • Recognition as one of Australia’s Best Workplaces™ by Great Place to Work®, a Family Friendly Workplace, a WORK180 endorsed employer.
  • A highly engaged, collaborative team where you’ll learn from exceptional talent.
  • 24 weeks paid parental leave for primary caregivers, four weeks for secondary caregivers, and six weeks paid gender-affirming care leave.
  • Regular hackathons, continuous learning opportunities, and wellbeing initiatives that support your mental, emotional, and physical health.
Job Description

What you’ll be doing

  • Own engineering execution for Machines4U, from product development and platform reliability to engineering practices and delivery cadence
  • Translate business priorities set by senior leadership into a clear, sequenced engineering roadmap and deliver against it
  • Lead a high-performing, distributed engineering team, and hold the behavioural and technical bar
  • Partner closely with product and carsales engineering counterparts to shape the Machines4U technology landscape and ensure engineering decisions serve commercial outcomes
  • Lead the progressive modernisation of Machines4U’s technology stack, balancing legacy platform stability with migration to scalable, secure and group-aligned modern systems.
  • Oversee key platform integrations and third-party technical dependencies, ensuring reliable interfaces across marketplace, CRM, payments, marketing, reporting and group systems.
  • Own engineering input into security, reliability and compliance practices, ensuring risks are understood, prioritised and remediated pragmatically.
  • Actively contribute to engineering practices, tooling, and culture across the carsales Engineering community
  • Provide clear, credible engineering perspectives to senior leadership, including assessments of risk, trade-offs, and capacity
Qualifications

What we're looking for

  • Proven experience leading engineering teams in a product-led, commercially-driven environment, ideally in a marketplace or B2B SaaS context
  • Strong technical depth across modern web stacks (backend and frontend), cloud infrastructure, and API design
  • Track record delivering practical engineering roadmaps that move the needle on business outcomes
  • Experience operating in a mixed legacy and modern technology environment, with a pragmatic approach to modernisation and risk reduction.
  • Natural relationship builder who can work effectively across functions, holding your own with stakeholders while staying accountable to a broader group
  • Credible operating range: from coding standards and incident response to roadmap trade-offs and senior leadership updates
  • Hands-on enough to review architecture, unblock engineers, and spot quality problems early
  • Pragmatic experience balancing delivery pace and meeting standards for security, reliability, and scalability

We know not everyone will meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If you’re excited about the role and believe you can make a difference, we’d love to hear from you.

Additional Information

A few other things we want you to know...

  • As an equal opportunity employer, Machines4U and carsales welcomes applications from individuals of all backgrounds, identities, abilities, and life experiences. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and supported.
  • If you have a disability or require adjustments to participate fully in the recruitment process, please let us know - we’re here to support you.
  • We understand the importance of work-life balance and offer flexible working arrangements, including part-time options. If you’d like to explore this, just ask during the process.
  • We’re also a proud Circle Back Initiative employer, which means we commit to responding to every applicant.
  • Successful candidates joining the Machines4U and carsales team will need to complete a National Police Record check and must have full-time Australian working rights.

So come and join our team - because every role is a big role in our plans to go big.

Level 2 IT Support Engineer

Arundel, QLD | Full Time | Permanent


Ready to take the next step in your IT career?

We're looking for a technically strong Level 2 IT Support Engineer to join an established Managed Services Provider (MSP) supporting businesses across Australia.

This is an opportunity to work in a collaborative technical team where you'll troubleshoot complex IT issues, manage escalations, and work across a diverse client portfolio spanning cloud, infrastructure, networking, and Microsoft technologies. If you enjoy solving problems, building strong client relationships, and expanding your technical expertise, this could be the role for you.


About the Company

Our client is an award-winning provider of managed IT, cyber security, cloud, and AI solutions, partnering with Australian businesses for more than 20 years. With offices across the Gold Coast, Brisbane, Sydney, and beyond, they support organisations across industries including construction, financial services, healthcare, real estate, and not-for-profit.

As a Microsoft Solutions Partner, they deliver tailored technology solutions including managed IT services, cyber security, cloud migration, AI enablement, and strategic vCIO consulting. Their focus is on becoming a trusted technology partner for their clients—not simply a help desk.

Their team is collaborative, supportive, and committed to continuous learning. You'll work alongside experienced engineers, gain exposure to a wide range of technologies, and be encouraged to develop your technical skills through ongoing training and certification.


About the Role

As a Level 2 IT Engineer, you'll provide advanced technical support across a broad range of client environments whilst managing escalated issues from Level 1. You'll take ownership of incidents from diagnosis through to resolution, contribute to infrastructure improvements, and deliver an outstanding customer experience.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Providing Level 2 technical support across desktop, server, network, and cloud environments

  • Managing escalated incidents and service requests, ensuring timely resolution

  • Troubleshooting Microsoft 365, Azure, Active Directory, Exchange, and Windows environments

  • Supporting networking technologies including firewalls, switches, VPNs, and wireless infrastructure

  • Maintaining accurate documentation and ticket updates within the PSA system

  • Building strong relationships with clients through professional communication and exceptional service

  • Assisting with infrastructure upgrades, migrations, and technology projects

  • Collaborating with Level 3 Engineers on complex technical issues when required


About You

To be successful in this role, you'll bring solid MSP experience, strong troubleshooting skills, and a proactive approach to customer support.

You'll ideally have:

  • 3+ years' experience in an MSP or similar IT support environment

  • Relevant IT qualifications or equivalent industry experience

  • Experience providing Level 2 technical support across Microsoft environments

  • Experience supporting networking technologies including switches, firewalls, VPNs, and Wi-Fi

  • Strong communication and customer service abilities

Highly regarded

  • Microsoft certifications (MS-900, AZ-900, MD-102, MS-102, AZ-104 or similar)

  • CompTIA, Cisco, or ITIL certifications

  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft 365, Azure, Active Directory, Exchange Online, and Windows Server

  • Experience using ConnectWise PSA and ConnectWise RMM

  • Exposure to Microsoft Intune, SharePoint, Defender, or Azure Virtual Desktop


Why You'll Love This Opportunity

  • Join an established and growing MSP with an outstanding reputation

  • Work across a broad range of technologies and client environments and gain exposure to cloud, cyber security, AI, and infrastructure projects

  • Ongoing technical mentoring from experienced senior engineers

  • Support for Microsoft certifications and continuous professional development

  • Collaborative team culture where your contribution is recognised and valued


If you're a technically capable IT professional who enjoys solving challenging problems and delivering exceptional service, we'd love to hear from you. Apply today and take the next step in your IT career!
